# Mexc.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the MEXC REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports Spot, sub-accounts, wallet/account endpoints, Futures, public market data, private trading, WebSocket streams, and CryptoExchange.Net shared interfaces.

Mexc.Net is a C# client for MEXC built on CryptoExchange.Net. It provides typed models, request signing, client-side rate limiting, WebSocket reconnection, `HttpResult<T>` / `WebSocketResult<T>` result handling, dependency injection, native AOT support, and shared multi-exchange abstractions. Current package version in this repository: 5.1.3. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/README.md): Overview, installation, supported features, usage examples, release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/Mexc.Net/): Full documentation hub including client reference, WebSocket subscriptions, dependency injection, and shared interfaces
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/docs/ai-api-map.md): Quick intent-to-method map for routing common user requests to the correct client member
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI/LLM guide with code patterns, endpoint map, common mistakes, and anti-hallucination rules
- [Claude/Codex Skill](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): Compact generation rules for assistants

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/tree/main/Examples/ai-friendly): Compact, fully runnable examples optimized for AI assistants and quick onboarding
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker, account balance, limit order placement, order status check
- [Futures Trading](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/02-futures.cs): Futures symbol format, leverage, market order, position retrieval, position close
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Spot public streams, spot listen-key private streams, futures private stream pattern, teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): Using CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis for exchange-agnostic code
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): HttpResult pattern, retry logic, common errors and filter handling
- [Full Examples Repository](https://github.com/JKorf/Mexc.Net/tree/main/Examples): Additional examples in this repository

## Critical Rules

- Use `MexcRestClient` for REST and `MexcSocketClient` for WebSocket.
- Install package `JK.Mexc.Net`, not `Mexc.Net`.
- Use `MexcCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- REST roots are `SpotApi` and `FuturesApi`; WebSocket roots are `SpotApi` and `FuturesApi`.
- Spot symbols look like `BTCUSDT`; futures symbols look like `BTC_USDT`.
- Spot private WebSocket subscriptions need a listen key from `restClient.SpotApi.Account.StartUserStreamAsync()`.
- MEXC exposes `MexcEnvironment.Live` and `MexcEnvironment.CreateCustom(...)`; do not invent a testnet environment.
- For multi-exchange code, use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` through `.SharedClient`.
